hero.sub: An honest Vietnamese coffee shop. Hand-poured phin one cup at a time, real fruit in every smoothie, and the warmest welcome on the block. Locals call us their morning routine.

search.no_results: Nothing matches that — try another word.

about.body: Cafe bà Chín opened as a single iron table on the sidewalk. Today we still hand-pour the phin, still keep the door open from sunrise, and still know your usual after the second visit. Every morning starts with an iced milk coffee and something soft playing in the background — that's the routine locals keep coming back for.

owner.title: Pulled by hand. Served by name.
owner.body: Twenty years of early mornings — opening before the neighborhood wakes up. What started as a single iron table on the sidewalk became the most-loved spot on the block, drawing regulars from near and far. Hand-pour the phin, Saigon milk coffee the family way. Guests keep coming back and spreading the word. I still open early every day — and learn one more name.

gallery.title: Regulars from everywhere.
gallery.sub: Customers and the owner — moments from the shop.

payment.title: Scan to pay.
payment.sub: VietQR, MoMo, ZaloPay — or cash at the counter.

promo: Enjoying your visit? Please leave us a review on Google — thank you so much! ⭐

footer.tag: Made with phin, condensed milk, and ice.
